_sources.discrepancies: Kansas has a strict photo ID requirement per NCSL. Voters without ID may cast a provisional ballot and return by the close of polls on the Monday following Election Day. Kansas primary is the first Tuesday in August per statute. Advanced (early in-person) voting available at county election offices.

earlyVoting.notes: Advance voting in person at county election offices starting 20 days before the election. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Advance voting in person at county election offices starting 20 days before the election. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Advance in-person voting at county election offices beginning 20 days before the general election. Mail ballot automatically sent to advance voters who have requested it. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.

acceptedIds: Student ID from a Kansas university, college, or technical school (with signature)
votingRules.expirationRule: Photo ID must not be expired. Exception: voters aged 65 or older may use an expired Kansas-issued photo ID. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
votingRules.impedimentDeclaration: Voters without acceptable photo ID may cast a provisional ballot. The ballot will be counted if the voter returns with acceptable ID by the close of polls on the Monday following the election.
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Kansas does not have a statewide prohibition on phones in polling places, but individual county election offices may have rules. Photographing your ballot is generally not permitted. Check with your county election office.
additionalRules: Closed primary: only voters registered with a party may vote in that party's primary.
No runoffs in Kansas — the candidate with the most votes wins.
Voters may vote by advance mail ballot without providing a reason.

eligibility: Any registered voter may request an advance mail ballot without providing a reason
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: 7 days before the election (received by county election office), or the close of advance voting for in-person advance
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by the county election office by the time polls close on Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Must arrive at the county election office by poll closing on Election Day. Postmarks do not count.
